fields.description:
## Context
115b/8c4f reconciled 13 benign fsck ORPHAN_EVENTs caused by a distributed write race: the Jira reconciler (env_id=reconciler) writes an assignee EDIT, a compaction in a DIFFERENT clone snapshots 3.7s-147s later without having observed that EDIT (so source_event_uuids omits it), then the reconciler re-emits an identical EDIT after the snapshot. fsck flags the pre-snapshot EDIT as orphaned. Live state is always correct (the post-snapshot duplicate drives it), so these are benign — but they recur and require manual retire-to-zero each time.

## Idea
A durable fix so the race stops generating orphans, e.g.: (a) compaction refuses to snapshot within N seconds of an unobserved remote write / syncs+refetches immediately before snapshotting; or (b) the reconciler's idempotent re-emit is suppressed when the value is unchanged; or (c) fsck learns to treat a pre-snapshot EDIT whose effect is superseded by an identical post-snapshot EDIT as non-orphan. Evaluate tradeoffs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause reproduced in a test (concurrent compaction + reconciler EDIT).
- [ ] A chosen mitigation implemented so the race no longer produces ORPHAN_EVENTs.
- [ ] Regression test pins that the previously-orphaning sequence now fscks clean.
